title: Extract a deep module owning the Copilot ChatCompletions wire primitives shared by both translators

body.summary: The Anthropic translator (src/routes/messages/) and the Codex/Responses translator (src/routes/responses/) both convert client requests into the SAME upstream ChatCompletionsPayload, and several transformations are duplicated near-verbatim. There is no neutral module that owns 'talking to the Copilot ChatCompletions shape', so each primitive is implemented (and must be fixed) twice.
body.problem: Duplicated transformations into the shared ChatCompletionsPayload: (1) thinking-budget resolution -- resolveThinkingBudget at non-stream-translation.ts:76-83 vs request-translation.ts:96-104; the keyword/anchor floor combine at non-stream:41-49 vs request:152-160; the 'thinking-on -> drop temperature/top_p, spread thinking_budget' block at non-stream:63-68 vs request:175-180 (effectively identical). (2) Tool lowering -- translateAnthropicToolsToOpenAI at non-stream:342-356 vs the 'function' case of lowerTools at request:242-257 (same target {type:'function',function:{name,description,parameters}}). (3) Content-part lowering -- mapContent at non-stream:290-340 vs mapResponsesContent at request:562-587 (identical hasImage gate -> join('\n\n') else build ContentPart[] with image_url). (4) Usage reshaping (input_tokens = prompt_tokens - cached_tokens) appears 3x in messages/ (non-stream:426-436, stream:44-54, stream:161-171) and once in responses/ (responses stream:129-140). The seam is already half-formed: detectKeywordBudget is shared, but responses/request-translation.ts:9 imports it by reaching ACROSS into the other translator's file (../messages/non-stream-translation) -- that cross-route import is the tell that the primitive has no neutral home.
body.solution: Introduce one deep module (e.g. src/services/copilot/wire/ or src/lib/copilot-wire.ts) that owns the Copilot ChatCompletions primitives: thinking-budget resolution (resolveThinkingBudget + the temp/top_p drop rule), function-tool lowering, content-part lowering (text/image), and usage reshaping (cached-token math). Both translators become thin adapters mapping their CLIENT vocabulary (Anthropic blocks / Responses items) onto these neutral primitives. Move the shared detectKeywordBudget into this module too so the cross-route import disappears.
body.benefits: Locality: the budget < max_tokens-1 invariant, the temperature/top_p drop rule, and the cached-token subtraction each live in ONE place instead of being fixed in 2-4. Leverage: a fourth wire format starts from primitives instead of copy-paste. Tests: the invariants get a single test surface instead of being re-tested per translator. The cross-file import smell (responses reaching into messages) is removed.
body.deletion_test: Deleting this proposed module would re-scatter the same 4 transformations back across both translators -- complexity reappears across callers, so the module earns its keep.
acceptance_criteria: resolveThinkingBudget exists exactly once and both translators call it
The temperature/top_p drop + thinking_budget spread rule exists exactly once
Usage reshaping (cached-token math) exists exactly once
bun test passes; bun run typecheck passes; bun run lint passes
out_of_scope: tool_choice translation (genuinely different between the two -- responses resolves synthetic/allowed_tools); leave per-translator
Streaming SSE state machine (tracked separately in ARCH-002)

progress.note: Task created from improve-codebase-architecture review. Duplication verified by reading both translator bodies (non-stream-translation.ts, request-translation.ts) and the three usage sites in messages/stream-translation.ts.

Started in worktree on branch task/ARCH-001-shared-copilot-wire-primitives. Baseline gates green: 111 pass/0 fail, typecheck 0, lint clean. Read all four target files + tests. CONFIRMED duplications: resolveThinkingBudget (non-stream:75-82 vs request:96-104), keyword/anchor floor combine (non-stream:40-48 vs request:152-159), temp/top_p drop + thinking_budget spread (non-stream:62-67 vs request:175-180), function-tool lowering (non-stream:337-344 vs request 'function' case 242-256), content-part lowering (mapContent non-stream:279-329 vs mapResponsesContent request:562-587), Anthropic cached-token usage math (non-stream:416-424, stream:45-53, stream:162-170). DEVIATION from task JSON claim (4): responses/stream-translation.ts:129-140 usage is NOT the same math -- it keeps input_tokens=prompt_tokens (no cached subtraction), adds total_tokens, nests cached under input_tokens_details (ResponsesUsage shape); confirmed by responses-stream.test.ts:419-437. Will unify the 3 Anthropic sites (criterion 5) and leave responses usage shape untouched. Plan: create src/lib/copilot-wire.ts owning the neutral primitives + move detectKeywordBudget there to kill the cross-route import.

Implemented in commit 25cc19a. Created src/services/copilot/chat-completions-wire.ts (placed beside create-chat-completions.ts where the ChatCompletions shape lives, preserving the routes->services import direction) owning: resolveThinkingBudget, combineBudgetFloor (keyword/explicit floor), applyThinkingBudget (temp/top_p drop + thinking_budget spread), detectKeywordBudget (+ KeywordSourceMessage, moved verbatim), buildFunctionTool (function-tool lowering), lowerContentParts (text/image hasImage gate via neutral WireContentPiece[]), toAnthropicUsage (cached-token subtraction). Both translators rewritten as thin adapters. Updated tests/thinking-keywords.test.ts import to the new home; added tests/copilot-wire.test.ts (20 cases) as the single test surface for the invariants. Acceptance criteria all met -- verified by grep that each primitive is defined exactly once and called from both translators (AC1-5), and that src/routes/responses/ has zero imports from ../messages/* (AC6). Gates green (AC7): bun test 131 pass/0 fail, bun run typecheck exit 0, bun run lint clean. SCOPE NOTE: function-tool lowering also routes the responses local_shell/custom synthetic tools through buildFunctionTool (same {type,function} target) so the shape truly exists once. tool_choice translation and the streaming SSE state machine were left untouched (out_of_scope). DEVIATION (carried from prior entry): the Responses streaming usage reshaping (responses/stream-translation.ts:129-140) was intentionally NOT unified -- it is a genuinely different shape (no cached subtraction; ResponsesUsage with total_tokens + input_tokens_details), so criterion 5's 'cached-token math' is the Anthropic-only subtraction now living once in toAnthropicUsage. Stopping here per TASK.md Rule 3; handing off to an independent verification subagent. NOT setting Done.

Verification by independent subagent. GATES (run fresh in worktree): bun test 131 pass / 0 fail / 229 expect across 8 files (baseline main was 111, so +20 net new with no regression -- the 502/401/429 [error] lines are expected error-path test output); bun run typecheck exit 0 (tsc no-emit); bun run lint exit 0 (eslint --cache, only a non-blocking baseline-browser-mapping freshness notice). PER-CRITERION (grep-confirmed exactly-once): AC1 resolveThinkingBudget defined only at chat-completions-wire.ts:28, called from non-stream:48 + request:153 -- PASS. AC2 applyThinkingBudget (temp/top_p drop + thinking_budget spread) defined only at wire:66, spread into both payloads (non-stream:65, request:174); no stray thinkingOn ternary remains -- PASS. AC3 buildFunctionTool defined only at wire:156; called from messages tool-lowering (non-stream:245) AND all three responses 'function'/local_shell/custom declaration cases (request:242,254,272). Remaining inline {type:'function'} sites are tool_choice (out_of_scope) and tool_CALL echoes in buildMessages (call_id/arguments history replay, a different shape -- not tool definitions) -- correctly left -- PASS. AC4 lowerContentParts + hasImage gate defined only at wire:178-182; both mapContent (non-stream:235) and mapResponsesContent (request:560) delegate; no duplicate hasImage in routes/ -- PASS. AC5 toAnthropicUsage defined only at wire:229; all 3 Anthropic sites delegate (non-stream:321, stream:45 with outputTokens:0, stream:152) -- PASS. AC6 grep 'messages/' in src/routes/responses/ => NONE; the ../messages/non-stream-translation import is gone -- PASS. AC7 gates green (above) -- PASS. INDEPENDENT CHECK OF THE USAGE DEVIATION: read responses/stream-translation.ts:129-140 directly -- it sets input_tokens = chunk.usage.prompt_tokens (NO cached subtraction), adds output_tokens, total_tokens, and nests cached under input_tokens_details.cached_tokens, matching the ResponsesUsage interface (responses-types.ts:212-217). This is a genuinely different shape from AnthropicUsage (which subtracts cached from prompt and surfaces cache_read_input_tokens flat), so toAnthropicUsage would produce wrong output here. Leaving it un-unified is CORRECT, not a missed dedup -- the implementer's deviation is sound. OUT-OF-SCOPE respected: tool_choice still resolved per-translator (responses synthetic/allowed_tools at request:323/339 untouched); streaming SSE state machine untouched (ARCH-002). VERDICT: all 7 acceptance criteria met, gates green, scope honored -> Done.
